headlineSummary: HN sentiment was mixed but leaned negative overall, with Google Gemini getting a small positive note on limited evidence while Anthropic and OpenAI drew more criticism over privacy and scraping concerns [E8][E4][E5][E9].

ranking.summary: OpenAI was viewed negatively on medium evidence, driven by complaints about aggressive crawling and disruptive scraping behavior, with one comment offering a mild comparison in its favor [E4][E5][E6].

Anthropic skewed negative with high-confidence criticism about scraping, closed-source concerns, and invasive data ingestion, though there was also some praise for supporting open source and Claude Code quality [E1][E2][E3][E4][E6][E7][E9].
Google Gemini had a mildly positive read on limited evidence, as one comment said it compared favorably to Google’s search AI summaries [E8].

evidence.excerpt: Anthropic invests $1.5M in the Python Software Foundation

annotations.rationale: The title is a factual announcement of Anthropic making a donation, without clear praise or criticism.

evidence.excerpt: Glad to see Anthropic continuing to invest in the longevity and quality of their open-source dependencies!

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly praises Anthropic's continued investment in open-source dependencies.

evidence.excerpt: I must be the only one in here who thinks $1.5M is a small sum compared to Anthropic's size and the amount of value they have gotten out of Python. Good press is cheaper than I thought.

annotations.rationale: This criticizes Anthropic's donation as small relative to its scale and implies it is a cheap PR move.

evidence.excerpt: I self host a small static website and a cgit instance on an e2-micro VPS from Google Cloud, and I have got around 8.5 million requests combined from openai and claude over around 160 days. They just infinitely crawl the cgit pages forever unless I block them!

annotations.rationale: The commenter blames OpenAI's crawler for massive, endless scraping traffic and frames it as harmful behavior.
The commenter blames Claude/Anthropic for massive, endless scraping traffic and frames it as harmful behavior.

evidence.excerpt: OpenAI was scraping it recently. I saw it looking at the event calendar, request after request to random days. I saw years 1000 through 3000 scroll by. I changed the response to their user agent to an access denied, but it still took about 4 hours for them to stop.

annotations.rationale: The commenter describes OpenAI as aggressively scraping a site in a disruptive way and not stopping promptly after being blocked.

evidence.excerpt: I did this as soon as I downloaded Claude Code as there was no way I was running it without any kind of sandboxing. I stopped using CC mainly because it's closed source and Codex and OpenCode work just a well.

annotations.rationale: The commenter says they would not run Claude Code unsandboxed and stopped using it mainly because it is closed source.
The commenter presents Codex as a satisfactory alternative that works as well for them.

evidence.excerpt: Claude Code and Cursor both do consistently great jobs.

annotations.rationale: The commenter directly praises Claude Code's performance.

evidence.excerpt: Google is really wrecking its brand with the search AI summaries thing, which is unbelievably bad compared to their Gemini offerings, including the free one.

annotations.rationale: Although criticizing Google's search AI summaries, the comment explicitly says Gemini offerings are much better by comparison.

evidence.excerpt: Meanwhile, Anthropic is openly pushing the ability to ingest our entire professional lives into their model

annotations.rationale: This explicitly criticizes Anthropic for pushing highly invasive data ingestion, framing it as a serious privacy problem.
